pr.title: Match Dolphin's hio based on changes from Dec 2001 SDK
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Replaced the remaining Dolphin HIO assembly implementation with a matching C translation based on later SDK behavior, specifically described as closer to the Dec 2001 SDK than the Revo/May 2001 source. The PR deleted asm/dolphin/hio/hio.s, added src/dolphin/hio/hio.c and src/dolphin/hio/hio.h, and updated build metadata so hio.c is compiled as a matching object with the EPI compiler path.
what_changed: Added a 335-line C implementation for Dolphin HIO in src/dolphin/hio/hio.c.

Added src/dolphin/hio/hio.h with HIOCallback and HIOEnumCallback typedefs plus public prototypes for HIOEnumDevices, HIOInit, HIOReadMailbox, HIOWriteMailbox, HIORead, HIOWrite, HIOReadAsync, HIOWriteAsync, and HIOReadStatus.
Implemented static HIO state and handlers in C: Chan, Dev, ExiCallback, TxCallback, RxCallback, ExtHandler, ExiHandler, DbgHandler, TxHandler, and RxHandler.
Rewired obj_files.mk from asm/dolphin/hio/hio.s.o to src/dolphin/hio/hio.c.o.
Changed configure.py so the hio object is marked Object(Matching, "dolphin/hio/hio.c") instead of Object(NonMatching, "dolphin/hio/hio.c"), and changed the containing entry from DolphinLib to Lib.
Added a Makefile rule to compile src/dolphin/hio/*.c with CC_EPI, matching nearby Dolphin subsystems such as card and pad.
smart_moves: Used SDK version selection as a matching tactic: the author explicitly avoided relying on May 2001/Revo HIO because it did not come close to Melee, and instead used material closer to Dec 2001 SDK behavior.

Kept hardware-facing control flow explicit in C: EXIProbeEx polling loops, EXILock/EXISelect/EXIImm/EXISync/EXIDeselect sequences, and early failure cleanup paths mirror the deleted assembly structure.
Used an accumulated error variable with err |= !EXI... calls, which is a compact way to model the assembly's repeated booleanization of return values and final success/failure return.
Preserved callback separation between EXI, TX, and RX paths with static function pointers and small trampoline handlers, matching the original assembly organization.

Handled the debug interrupt path separately through DbgHandler using __PIRegs[0] = 0x1000, OSClearContext, OSSetCurrentContext, __OSSetInterruptHandler, and __OSUnmaskInterrupts, instead of flattening it into the regular EXI callback path.

Updated all build lists at once: compiler selection, configure object status, and obj_files.mk object routing were changed together so the new C file actually replaces the old assembly in the matching build.
decomp_lessons: For Dolphin SDK code, the closest available SDK drop may not be the newest or most obvious source; compare SDK date behavior against Melee before porting names or structure.
When replacing an asm object, update both the object list and the configure metadata; otherwise a C file can exist without actually replacing the assembly in the linked text section.
Small SDK libraries may require the same compiler override as nearby Dolphin code. Here src/dolphin/hio/*.c was explicitly assigned CC_EPI in the Makefile.

Hardware protocol functions often match better when command constants and transfer sizes are left visible: examples in this PR include 0x60000000 for mailbox read, 0xC0000000 for mailbox write, 0x20000000 for HIO read, 0xA0000000 for HIO write, and 0x40000000 for status read.
Empty do-while polling loops can be necessary to recover assembly shape for EXI readiness checks, as shown by repeated `do { } while (EXIProbeEx(...) == 0);` patterns.
Callback typedefs in the local header made the HIO public API readable while keeping the handler globals static to the C file.
naming_conventions: Public HIO APIs use the Dolphin SDK-style HIO prefix and UpperCamelCase function names: HIOInit, HIOReadMailbox, HIOWriteMailbox, HIOReadAsync, HIOWriteAsync.
Internal interrupt and transfer handlers use short descriptive UpperCamelCase names matching the old assembly symbols: ExtHandler, ExiHandler, DbgHandler, TxHandler, RxHandler.
Static state names mirror the assembly globals and SDK style rather than project-local snake_case: Chan, Dev, ExiCallback, TxCallback, RxCallback.
The header typedef names are SDK-style: HIOCallback for void callbacks and HIOEnumCallback for enumeration callbacks taking an s32 channel.
The new header uses the guard token _DOLPHIN_HIO_H_ and exposes a macro `HIO_DEV Dev`; the dump does not show review discussion explaining this macro.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted branch-heavy assembly into straightforward early returns and cleanup blocks while preserving EXI call order.
Used boolean returns as 0/1 rather than C99 true/false literals, consistent with the generated code style in the diff.
Used explicit unsigned comparisons/constants such as `__OSGetDIConfig() == 0xFFU`, `id != 0x1010000`, and `(u32)(id + 0xFEFF0000) == 0U` to model original compare/addis behavior.
Used `err |= !function_call` accumulation to match repeated failure aggregation across EXIImm, EXISync, EXIDma, EXIDeselect, and final `return !err` patterns.
Preserved asynchronous transfer shape by leaving EXIDeselect and EXIUnlock to TxHandler/RxHandler after EXIDma callbacks rather than doing synchronous cleanup.
Marked hio.c as Matching in configure.py only after replacing the assembly object, providing evidence the new source matched sufficiently for the project.

review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer approved and said: 'Not saying it needs to be done in this PR, but I just wanted to make sure you're aware of #1409. Nice work.'

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.response: Author replied they were aware of #1409 and chose existing Revo-provided material cautiously because Revo HIO was May 2001 and did not come close to matching; Melee appeared closer to Dec 2001, possibly an intermediate Nov 2001 SDK.

follow_up_queries: Compare PR #1409 against PR #1437 to understand overlapping Dolphin HIO or SDK-source work.
Search for other Dolphin SDK files where May 2001 source does not match Melee but Dec 2001 or intermediate SDK behavior does.
Check whether other src/dolphin subdirectories require CC_EPI overrides when assembly is replaced with matching C.
Audit HIO header conventions, especially the _DOLPHIN_HIO_H_ guard and HIO_DEV macro, if later HIO work touches public includes.
